commit.message:
Harden allocation algorithm against SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED contention (#9)

* Initial plan

* feat: retry allocation processor on SELECT FOR UPDATE lock contention

When findByIdsForUpdate returns fewer locked rows than expected (SKIP
LOCKED skipped rows held by concurrent transactions), the processor now
retries up to MAX_RETRIES=3 times with a 50ms back-off instead of
immediately marking the allocation as FAILED.

Key logic:
- hasContention = lockedCandidates.size < candidateIds.size
- Contention → retry; no contention but algorithm fails → fail immediately
- Phase 1 (findByState) is re-executed each attempt to reflect equipment
  committed by concurrent transactions (PostgreSQL READ COMMITTED)

Also updates AllocationProcessorTest:
- Replaces old single-attempt contention test with a MAX_RETRIES test
- Adds test for successful allocation on retry (contention resolves)
- Adds test for immediate failure without retrying on genuine shortage

commit.message:
fix(backup): create velero-ui-passphrase secret and disable upgradeCRDs hook

- Add encrypted velero-ui-passphrase.sops.yaml so Flux can create the
  JWT passphrase secret referenced by the velero-ui HelmRelease env var
  AUTH_SECRET_PASSPHRASE. The file was previously empty, causing the pod
  to fail with CreateContainerConfigError on every deploy.

- Set upgradeCRDs: false in the velero HelmRelease. Flux already handles
  CRD updates via upgrade.crds: CreateReplace. The chart's own pre-upgrade
  hook job (velero-upgrade-crds) was timing out repeatedly on small
  clusters, causing Helm to roll back on every reconcile loop.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  recorder, skill evolver, pattern aggregator, auto-generator, PRM scorer
- Context Engineering (5 features): JIT context loader, relevance decay,
  memory GC, context quality scorer, scaffold benchmark

WildClawBench. Maps directly onto our marketplace architecture.

* Refactor Kitty keyboard protocol detection & enablement

Centralize Kitty keyboard enable/disable logic in KittyKeyboardProtocolDetector, removing it from AnsiOutput. Simplify KittyKeyboardCapabilities by replacing SupportedFlags and EnabledFlags with a single Flags property. Update MainLoopCoordinator and driver to use the new API, and make KittyKeyboardCapabilities settable on IDriver. Update all usages and tests to use the new Flags property. Remove obsolete code and tests from AnsiOutput. Improve logging and tracing for Kitty protocol detection and driver initialization. Update Keys scenario and UICatalogRunnable to display Kitty protocol status using the new approach.

* Improve kitty keyboard protocol negotiation & cleanup

Enhances driver and main loop to reliably enable kitty keyboard mode on startup (with post-enable detection of actual flags) and disable it on shutdown before disposing output. Updates KittyKeyboardProtocolDetector to confirm and store enabled flags. Reduces debug output by commenting trace lines. Updates docs to clarify negotiation and shutdown flow. Adds/updates tests to verify enable/disable sequences and capability updates.

* Add tests for mixed Kitty and legacy input deduplication

Added a new test region to KittyKeyboardPipelineTests to ensure no duplicate KeyDown events are raised when both Kitty CSI-u sequences and legacy printable characters are received for the same keypress. Tests cover generic printable keys, Portuguese keyboard characters, and Kitty sequences with associated text, addressing terminal behaviors that emit both input types.

* Fixes #4918 - Suppress duplicate keydown for Kitty+legacy printable input

Added suppression logic in AnsiInputProcessor to prevent duplicate keydown events when both Kitty CSI-u and legacy printable sequences are received for the same key. Introduced virtual methods in InputProcessorImpl for customizable suppression. Updated related tests for style consistency.

commit.message:
fix: replace network_count proxy with HTML scraping for dependents

network_count from the GitHub REST API is the fork-network count,
not the dependents count. This caused forks (e.g. rajbos/microsoft-
todo-mcp-server) to show the upstream fork-network size (23) instead
of their real dependent count (0).

Changes:
- Return 0 immediately for fork repos (dependency graph is disabled
  for forks, so dependents is always 0)
- For non-forks, scrape /network/dependents?dependent_type=REPOSITORY
  and parse the count from the HTML tab link
- Guard against login-redirect pages (unauthenticated private repos)
- Forward GITHUB_TOKEN as Authorization header when available
- Add comprehensive tests covering forks, large counts with commas,
  same-line vs multi-line HTML, non-200 responses, and login redirects

commit.message:
fix: active device must increment current_playable_index on track completion

Logs confirmed that neither update_playing_status(progress=duration)
nor sync_state_from_eov advances the queue — Ynison is a state-sync
protocol, not a command protocol. The active device is responsible
for advancing the queue index.

Track completion now:
1. update_playing_status(progress=duration) — report track end
2. update_player_state(index+1, progress=0) — advance queue
3. sync_state_from_eov(entity_id) — replenish radio/wave

This matches reference implementations (go-yaynison, FozerG).

`DefaultProcessRunner` with a slim `ShellExecutionOptions` data class,
breaking the back-reference from `Tasks/Shell` to `Configuration`.

properties (with null validation)
- **`IProcessRunner`** — signature uses `ShellExecutionOptions` instead
of `ShellTaskSpecification`
- **`DefaultProcessRunner`** — updated to use `options.Silent`
- **`IShellInvoker`** — signature uses `ShellExecutionOptions` instead
of `ShellTaskSpecification`
- **`PowerShellInvoker`** / **`ShInvoker`** — create
`ShellExecutionOptions` from spec properties

files

commit.message:
Add montage video player with automatic active song sync from timestamps (#17)

Extracts the montage video and embedded player from PR #16, with the key
addition of automatic active song synchronization based on video
timestamps. When the montage plays on the dashboard, `activeSong`
updates via `useSyncedState` so all connected voters see the current
song highlighted in real time on their vote page.

### Constants & data model
- `DEFAULT_ESC_YEAR` (2026) and `ESC_MONTAGE_DATA` (year-keyed map
bundling YouTube video ID + timestamp entries) in `constants.ts`
- `escYear` added to `Game` interface, migration 004, `createGame`
(defaults to `DEFAULT_ESC_YEAR`), and `getGameByToken`
- Montage YouTube ID and timestamps are derived from the year at runtime
via `ESC_MONTAGE_DATA[escYear]`, ensuring they stay in sync and
future-proofing for other ESC years

### Montage player (`montage-player.tsx`)
- Embeds `react-player@3.4.0` (v3 — HTML5 video element API, not v2's
`url`/`onProgress`)
- Receives `timestamps` as a prop (decoupled from global constants)
- On `timeupdate`, maps `currentTime` → country via linear lookup over
the provided timestamps
- Calls `onSongChange` only when the country actually changes, avoiding
unnecessary synced state writes

```tsx
// Dashboard wires the player's song changes to useSyncedState
<MontagePlayer
  youtubeId={montageData.youtubeId}
  timestamps={montageData.timestamps}
  onSongChange={(country) => setActiveSong(country)}
  onEnded={() => { setIsMontageActive(false); setActiveSong(null); }}
/>
```

### Dashboard controls
- "▶️ Play Montage" / "⏹ Stop Montage" toggle (visible when
`ESC_MONTAGE_DATA` has an entry for the game's `escYear`)
- Clicking a manual song button while montage is active stops the
montage
- Stop button clears both montage and active song state

### Not included from PR #16
TV Display page (`/:token/tv`), remote control features (`tvPlayback`,
`tvProgress`, `tvMode` synced state channels) — left for future.

---------

NOT sufficient — Ynison echoes back the same track/index without
advancing. The active device must request EOV sync.

an empty string. Empty string forced a full queue reset that broke
Yandex's queue ownership. With the real entity_id, EOV advances
